evidence: Major packages (agent/, gateway/, hermes_cli/, tools/, cron/, honcho_integration/, environments/) each map to a clear domain capability with predictable naming
gateway/__init__.py defines an explicit __all__ with curated public surface; gateway/platforms/ follows uniform one-file-per-platform pattern with ADDING_A_PLATFORM.md guide
Tool registry pattern (tools/registry.py) is consistently used by all 30+ tools, with model_tools.py as a thin orchestration layer
cli.py (5,162 lines), run_agent.py (5,725 lines), and gateway/run.py (4,067 lines) are monolithic files concentrating multiple responsibilities each
AGENTS.md project structure lists acp_adapter/ which does not exist and omits honcho_integration/, skills/, demo/, scripts/, hermes_time.py, utils.py, rl_cli.py, mini_swe_runner.py among others

13 top-level Python files (17K lines total) create a flat root with mixed production (cli.py, run_agent.py, hermes_state.py) and research code (batch_runner.py, trajectory_compressor.py, rl_cli.py, mini_swe_runner.py)
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Three monolithic files (cli.py, run_agent.py, gateway/run.py) each exceed 4K lines and concentrate multiple responsibilities, making change boundaries wide and ownership unclear within each. AGENTS.md structure documentation is materially inaccurate. Research/RL code is intermixed with production code at the top level without clear separation.

high_level_elegance.dimension_character: The codebase has a sound domain-aligned package structure: gateway/, tools/, hermes_cli/, agent/, cron/, and honcho_integration/ each own a clear capability, and within those packages decomposition is generally clean (e.g., one file per platform adapter, one file per tool, explicit __init__.py exports). However, the three largest files (run_agent.py, cli.py, gateway/run.py) are 4-6K line monoliths that concentrate multiple responsibilities, undermining the otherwise good package-level decomposition. The project structure documentation in AGENTS.md is stale -- it contains a phantom directory and omits many real modules. Research/RL code (batch_runner, trajectory_compressor, rl_cli, environments/) coexists at the top level with production agent code without clear boundary markers.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: Package-level structure is domain-aligned and a new engineer could navigate the top-level directories confidently (pulling toward 80+). But three monolithic files totaling 15K lines blur ownership within their respective domains, and the inaccurate AGENTS.md means the 'map' of the codebase cannot be trusted. Research code intermixed at the root adds cognitive overhead. These issues create enough friction that a developer would regularly encounter 'why is this here?' moments, placing this in the 70-75 range -- mixed quality where good structure is undermined by concentrated files and documentation drift.

issues.summary: AGENTS.md project structure lists nonexistent acp_adapter/ and omits many real directories and files
evidence: AGENTS.md line 57 references 'acp_adapter/' which does not exist in the repository

Missing from AGENTS.md structure: honcho_integration/, skills/, demo/, scripts/, hermes_constants.py, hermes_time.py, utils.py, toolset_distributions.py, trajectory_compressor.py, rl_cli.py, mini_swe_runner.py
agent/ listing omits anthropic_adapter.py, autoreply.py, insights.py, redact.py; hermes_cli/ listing omits 16 of 27 files; gateway/ listing omits 10 of 13 files
issues.suggestion: Update AGENTS.md project structure to remove acp_adapter/ and add the missing directories and key files. For large packages, listing representative files plus '...' is fine, but the top-level structure must be accurate.

issues.summary: cli.py (5,162 lines) concentrates interactive REPL, slash command handlers, and UI rendering in one file
evidence: cli.py contains the HermesCLI class starting at line 991 with all slash command handlers (_handle_rollback_command, _handle_paste_command, _handle_prompt_command, etc.)
The file includes prompt_toolkit TUI setup, threading, streaming display logic, and agent lifecycle management
Slash command handlers could be extracted to a dedicated module (similar to how hermes_cli/commands.py already defines command metadata)
issues.suggestion: Extract slash command handler methods from HermesCLI into a separate module (e.g., cli_commands.py or a cli/ package) that the HermesCLI class delegates to. This would reduce cli.py to REPL orchestration and TUI management, which is its core responsibility.

issues.summary: run_agent.py (5,725 lines) remains a monolith despite agent/ extraction, concentrating the entire AIAgent class
evidence: agent/__init__.py explicitly states these are 'extracted modules from run_agent.py' -- the extraction started but left the 5.7K line core intact
AIAgent class (line 205) contains conversation loop, model selection, context management, tool dispatch, streaming, and session persistence
The agent/ package has 12 focused modules averaging ~350 lines each, showing the target granularity is achievable
issues.suggestion: Continue the agent/ extraction: move conversation loop logic, streaming/response handling, and session persistence into focused modules within agent/. The AIAgent class should become a thin orchestrator that composes these extracted modules.

issues.summary: gateway/run.py (4,067 lines) concentrates message routing, slash commands, model resolution, and cron ticker
evidence: GatewayRunner class (line 215) handles adapter lifecycle, message dispatch, slash command processing, model resolution, cron ticking, and control API integration
Other gateway modules are well-decomposed (config.py 483L, session.py 813L, delivery.py 344L, pairing.py 282L) showing the target granularity
The file has 4,067 lines while its sibling modules average ~300 lines
issues.suggestion: Extract gateway slash command handlers into gateway/commands.py and model/provider resolution logic into gateway/model_resolution.py. The GatewayRunner should focus on adapter lifecycle and message routing.

issues.summary: 13 top-level Python files mix production agent code with research/RL infrastructure without clear separation
evidence: Production code (cli.py, run_agent.py, model_tools.py, hermes_state.py) sits alongside research/RL code (batch_runner.py, trajectory_compressor.py, rl_cli.py, mini_swe_runner.py, toolset_distributions.py) at the top level
A new engineer cannot tell from directory listing alone which files are production vs research infrastructure
The environments/ package is also RL-specific but gets no special treatment in the project structure
issues.suggestion: Move batch_runner.py, trajectory_compressor.py, rl_cli.py, mini_swe_runner.py, and toolset_distributions.py into a dedicated research/ or training/ package. This would clarify the production vs research boundary and reduce top-level clutter from 13 to 8 files.

root_causes: Organic growth pattern: core files (run_agent.py, cli.py, gateway/run.py) grew incrementally and extraction into packages started (agent/) but didn't complete
Documentation maintenance lag: AGENTS.md structure was written at one point in time and not kept in sync with ongoing changes
likely_symptoms: flat_root_mixed_concerns is partly a symptom of the organic growth root cause -- research features were added alongside production code without a packaging decision

add.description: Major packages (agent/, gateway/, hermes_cli/, tools/, cron/, honcho_integration/) each own a single domain capability. A new engineer can predict what each directory contains from the name alone.
gateway/platforms/ follows a uniform one-file-per-platform convention with a shared base.py ABC and an ADDING_A_PLATFORM.md guide, making the extension pattern obvious.
All 30+ tools self-register via tools/registry.py at import time. model_tools.py is a thin orchestration layer. This is the single consistent architectural pattern for tool management.
gateway/__init__.py defines an explicit __all__ exporting config types, session management, and delivery routing -- a curated API surface that matches the package's stated responsibility.

cli.py (5.2K lines), run_agent.py (5.7K lines), and gateway/run.py (4K lines) each concentrate multiple responsibilities. The agent/ extraction started but left the core intact. These files are the primary source of ownership ambiguity within their domains.

hermes_cli/main.py is the argparse subcommand dispatcher ('hermes setup', 'hermes gateway', etc.) while cli.py is the interactive REPL. main.py delegates 'hermes chat' to cli.py. This is intentional but means CLI responsibilities span two locations.
The documented structure includes a nonexistent acp_adapter/ directory and omits many real directories and files, making it an unreliable map of the codebase.

batch_runner.py, trajectory_compressor.py, rl_cli.py, mini_swe_runner.py, and toolset_distributions.py are RL/research infrastructure that sit at the project root alongside production code without clear separation.
